_meta.author: scorecard-refine-local cron
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize local TX batch: San Marcos City Council (Paselk P2, Gonzalez P5, Mendoza P1, Scott P4) + Hays County Sheriff Hipolito. Per-official parallel CITED research; LOCAL tier; social-media/minutes protocol. Every TRUE/FALSE cites a source; null where no evidence; never inferred from party. Roster currency verified for all 5 (all currently seated). Office fix: Shane Scott is Place 4 only — he is NOT Mayor Pro Tem (that is Alyssa Garza, Place 3).

profile.background: Small-business owner (landscaping/irrigation, ~12 yrs); former Planning & Zoning commissioner; Chamber board member. Won the Dec 13, 2025 Place 2 runoff (54.4%) vs incumbent Saul Gonzales; sworn in Dec 22, 2025; term 2025-2028.

josh-paselk.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-17 (CITED). Roster verified: seated San Marcos City Council Place 2, term 2025-2028 (won Dec 2025 runoff). San Marcos council is nonpartisan; no party affiliation found. Self-identifies as Christian on his campaign site ('husband, father, Christian') but that is a faith identity statement, not a policy position. Researched all 10 RESOLUTE topics + city/Chamber/campaign sources: NO documented position found on life, marriage/sexuality, schools, Christian liberty, election integrity, immigration, guns, or affirmative pro-police. Only on-record items are a data-center rezoning vote (changed to no per community consensus, cited revenue concern) and generic 'fiscal discipline' language in the Chamber questionnaire — neither maps to a scoreable rubric cell. All applicable local cells null (honest: no evidence). roster_verified, not evidence_local, because zero findable policy positions.

profile.background: School-Based Law Enforcement Officer (Del Valle ISD Police Dept); former neighborhood commissioner. Won the Dec 14, 2024 Place 5 runoff (50.72%, by 12 votes); sworn in Dec 23, 2024; term 2024-2027. Nonpartisan seat; previously ran in the Democratic primary for Hays County Constable Pct 1 (Democratic lean).

lorenzo-gonzalez.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-17 (CITED). Roster verified: seated San Marcos City Council Place 5, term 2024-2027. Scored cells: (1) economic q2 TRUE — voted AGAINST the staff-recommended 67.69 cent tax rate AND against the 65.15 cent compromise rate that passed 5-2, favoring the No-New-Revenue rate (~64.96 cents); 'Homeowners, renters and small businesses will continue to shoulder the cost of a growing city' (fiscal restraint, conservative direction). (2) public_justice q0 TRUE — affirmative pro-police: 'As a member of law enforcement... I fully support the need for strong police and sheriff departments to maintain public safety'; he is himself a sworn school police officer (with an accountability caveat re: bad actors). (3) biblical_marriage q4 FALSE — in the Chamber questionnaire he named 'the LGBTQ+ community' among groups he would prioritize for city small-business help, i.e. an affirmative use of LGBTQ+ identity in public policy (contrary to opposing promotion of LGBTQ+ identity in public policy). Context (not scored): said 'health care is a human right'; voted with the 5-2 majority to REJECT the 2025 Gaza/Israel ceasefire resolution. NO EVIDENCE on life, schools, Christian liberty, election integrity, immigration, guns, or refuse-state-overreach -> null.

2.note: Voted against both the 67.69 cent staff rate and the 65.15 cent compromise rate that passed 5-2; favored the lower No-New-Revenue rate, citing burden on homeowners/renters/small business.

0.note: Affirmative pro-police statement: 'I fully support the need for strong police and sheriff departments to maintain public safety'; he is a sworn school police officer. (Accountability caveat re: bad actors, but affirmative backing.)

4.note: Named 'the LGBTQ+ community' among groups he would prioritize for city small-business assistance — affirmative use of LGBTQ+ identity in public policy.

profile.background: 8th-generation San Marcos native; IT/analytic-engineering career; former Planning & Zoning commissioner. First elected Nov 2022; re-elected Nov 2025 (56.77%); term 11/17/2025-11/30/2028. Nonpartisan seat.

matthew-mendoza.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-17 (CITED). Roster verified: seated San Marcos City Council Place 1, term 2025-2028 (re-elected Nov 2025). Researched all 10 RESOLUTE topics + Legistar/Chamber/press. Two on-record items, neither scored: (a) economic — voted against the initial 67.69 cent max tax rate BUT his stated rationale was 'find new revenue sources' (not cut spending), and his platform is interventionist (workforce-housing committee, housing-repair funding, small-business grants); this is not the fiscal-restraint posture the cell rewards (contrast Gonzalez/Scott who backed the No-New-Revenue/lower rate outright) -> null, not TRUE. (b) refuse_state_overreach — named-defendant in his official capacity in State of Texas v. City of San Marcos defending the voter-passed Prop A marijuana-decriminalization ordinance against AG Paxton; the 15th Court of Appeals (Apr 2025) held the ordinance VIOLATES state law, so this is resistance to a VALID state law (not an 'unconstitutional state mandate') and directionally pro-decriminalization -> does not satisfy the cell -> null. Voting a budget that funds police does not count as backing police (skill high-bar) -> public_justice null. NO EVIDENCE on life, marriage, schools, Christian liberty, elections, immigration, guns -> null.

profile.background: Owner of German Elite Autos (San Marcos dealership, 30+ yrs); Texas State University B.A. Criminal Justice. Long-serving councilman (2010-2012, 2012-2015, 2020-2023, re-elected Nov 2023 ~64%); current Place 4 term 2023-2026. Small-government/'fewer restrictions, less red tape' governing philosophy. NOTE: NOT Mayor Pro Tem (that is Alyssa Garza, Place 3) — prior office label corrected.

shane-scott.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-17 (CITED). Roster verified: seated San Marcos City Council Place 4, term 2023-2026. OFFICE CORRECTED: he is Place 4 only, not Mayor Pro Tem (Mayor Pro Tem is Alyssa Garza, appointed Nov 2023). Scored cells: (1) economic q2 TRUE — consistent multi-year pattern of voting AGAINST property-tax-rate increases (dissented on FY2021-22, FY2022-23, lone dissenter on the FY2024-25 max rate), small-government posture. (2) refuse_state_overreach q2 TRUE — during 2020-21 opposed additional local COVID restrictions, pro-reopening to protect small business: 'Our small businesses, especially, have been so destroyed... out of business,' argued the city should rely on vaccine rollout rather than tighter rules (protected citizens from lockdown overreach). public_justice q0 NULL — only a procedural objection (called a public-meet-and-confer-negotiations amendment 'an overreach'); NOT an affirmative pro-police endorsement or partnership program. A 'Shane Scott' federal firearms license listing is unverified as him and is not a policy position -> self_defense null. NO EVIDENCE on life, marriage, schools, Christian liberty, elections, immigration -> null.

2.note: Consistent multi-year record of voting against property-tax-rate increases (FY2021-22, FY2022-23 dissents; lone dissenter on the FY2024-25 max proposed rate); small-government fiscal restraint.
Opposed additional local COVID-19 restrictions in 2020-21, citing harm to small business; favored relying on the vaccine rollout over tighter mandates — protected citizens from lockdown overreach.

profile.background: 39th Sheriff of Hays County. APD 1998-2021 (retired Sergeant, 23 yrs); HCSO Community Outreach Division from 2021; led HCSO Fentanyl Awareness campaign. Elected Nov 5, 2024 as a Republican (52.69% vs Democrat Alex Villalobos), endorsed by retiring Sheriff Gary Cutler; sworn in Jan 2025; first term 2024-2028.

anthony-hipolito-jr.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-17 (CITED). Roster + PARTY verified: seated Hays County Sheriff, confirmed Republican (won Nov 2024, 52.69%), term 2024-2028. Scored cells: (1) public_justice q0 TRUE — affirmatively pro-law-enforcement: 'committed to keeping Hays County the safest county in the country,' touts putting high-level drug dealers in federal prison, runs SRO/fentanyl programs. (2) public_justice q1 TRUE — anti-soft-on-crime: on student threats, 'If we have to make an example out of these kids by putting them in jail, we're going to continue to do that because the message has to be clear' (jail-as-deterrent, tough-on-crime). (3) border_immigration q2 TRUE — under TX SB 8 chose the 287(g) warrant-service-officer model, honors ICE holds, and HCSO supported an Operation Lone Star-related immigration action; cooperative with ICE, no sanctuary policy ('an easy decision... the least intrusive of the three options... something we already do'). NULLs: q1 border (no 'mandatory deportation of all' statement; routine framing); refuse_state_overreach (he is NOT a constitutional sheriff — readily complied with SB 8); self_defense ('gun on every campus' is about armed SRO deputies, not civilian 2A; no constitutional-carry/red-flag statement); family (SRO/school-safety, not school-choice/parental-rights/CRT cells); victim-restitution / public-health / magistrate-sword cells (no explicit statement). NO EVIDENCE on life, marriage, Christian liberty, election integrity -> null.

0.note: Affirmatively pro-law-enforcement: 'committed to keeping Hays County the safest county in the country,' touts federal prosecutions of drug dealers, runs SRO/fentanyl-awareness programs.

1.note: Anti-soft-on-crime, jail-as-deterrent: 'If we have to make an example out of these kids by putting them in jail, we're going to continue to do that.'

2.note: Entered a 287(g) warrant-service-officer agreement under TX SB 8, honors ICE holds, HCSO supported an Operation Lone Star action — cooperative with ICE, no sanctuary policy.
